query_normalized: quantifying the complexity of knot diagrams via crossing number and braid index mathematics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) as it studies the same mathematical constructs (crossing number and braid index) that the user is asking about, and criterion (b) as it measures both variables on the same domain (knot/link theory). The paper directly investigates the relationship between these two complexity measures in knot theory, making it canonical prior work for any literature review on quantifying knot diagram complexity via these invariants.
